plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet the single-name mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure for capital preservation.
Increase QQQI to 25% for enhanced income and stability.
Buy XLV at 10% to diversify into healthcare, a strong scanner setup.
Maintain a significant cash buffer by reducing overall exposure.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets approximately 1.5% per month, prioritizing capital preservation. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, aiming for lower drawdown. Expected volatility is much lower, but so is potential upside.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to align with concentration limits and reduce gross leverage.
Sell SOXL entirely as it is a flagged holding and a leveraged ETF.
Reduce SPAL to 3% to keep total leveraged ETFs below 5%.
Add to CRWD, increasing to 15%, to press a winning AI position.
Buy LRCX at 8% to add a high-scoring semiconductor name.
Buy XLV at 7% to diversify into a strong healthcare setup.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets approximately 2.5% per month, focusing on risk-first growth. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while blending broad index with quality tech. It aims for a moderate risk/reward profile.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to comply with concentration limits and manage gross leverage.
Sell SOXL entirely, as it is a flagged holding and below its stop.
Trim SMH to 10% to reduce exposure to a downtrending sector.
Add to CRWD, increasing to 20%, to press a strong AI winner.
Buy LRCX at 10% and ASML at 8% to concentrate in high-scoring semiconductor setups.
Add to SPAL, increasing to 7%, to utilize leveraged exposure within limits.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets approximately 4% per month, aiming for stretch growth. It leans into leverage and concentration in high-scoring setups, which amplifies both upside and drawdown. Be aware of daily decay in leveraged ETFs and strict stop discipline is critical.

plan: Trim SPY to 15% and QQQ to 10% to reduce broad index exposure and free up cash.
Sell all SMH and CRWD to exit high-beta sector and single-name exposure.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate all leveraged ETF holdings.
Increase QQQI to 40% to maximize income allocation for steady distributions.
Maintain a significant cash buffer for stability and flexibility.
income.read: This income plan targets approximately 1% per month, prioritizing steady monthly distributions. It significantly reduces growth-oriented and leveraged holdings, aiming for low drawdown. Distributions are variable, and NAV can still fall in a selloff.
